title: Unlocking Scientific Solutions for Africa: The Jennifer Ward Oppenheimer Research Grant 2026
summary: The Jennifer Ward Oppenheimer Research Grant (JWO Research Grant) is a prestigious research funding opportunity designed to empower early-career scientists whose work addresses critical scientific and environmental challenges in Africa. Established to honor the legacy of the late Jennifer Ward Oppenheimer and her passion for science, the environment, and African development, this grant supports innovative research with meaningful impact across the continent. With a strong focus on driving evidence-based solutions, the JWO Research Grant invites applications from researchers who are committed to tackling pressing issues—from biodiversity conservation to climate resilience and sustainable development. This grant not only provides financial support but also fosters a collaborative research community, offering recipients a platform to share insights and scale their work. Why the JWO Research Grant Matters Scientific research is essential for addressing complex problems that affect societies and ecosystems. In Africa, where diverse environmental and social challenges intersect, research plays a pivotal role in informing policy, guiding sustainable practices, and enhancing community well-being. The JWO Research Grant recognizes this need and seeks to catalyze impactful research by supporting early-career scientists with visionary ideas. The grant is especially significant for researchers who may face barriers in accessing funding. By offering substantial financial backing and recognition, the JWO Research Grant helps bridge the gap between innovative research proposals and real-world implementation. Previous recipients have conducted transformative work in areas such as soil biodiversity, ecosystem management, and climate-adaptive solutions. Application Details and Deadline The application window for the 2026 JWO Research Grant opened on 23 March 2026 and closes on 1 May 2026. Full proposals for invited candidates will be due by 3 July 2026, with interviews for the top finalists scheduled for August 17–21, 2026. The successful recipient will be announced at the annual Oppenheimer Research Conference in October 2026. Grant Benefits Selected recipients of the JWO Research Grant receive a financial award of USD $150,000, which can be used to advance their research goals. Beyond funding, recipients gain visibility within the international research community and access to networks that can support future collaborations and professional growth. Previous winners have leveraged this platform to expand their research scope and contribute to policy discussions in their fields. What the Grant Supports The JWO Research Grant is open to proposals that address scientific questions with clear relevance to African challenges. Past projects have focused on topics such as: Strengthening freshwater ecosystems for climate resilience Soil biodiversity conservation for climate-smart agriculture Marine conservation and community engagement Space science applications for disaster management Plant-pollinator network resilience and food security Coastal ecosystem restoration for livelihoods and biodiversity These diverse themes reflect the grant’s commitment to supporting interdisciplinary and high-impact research that aligns with Africa’s development priorities. Who Should Apply Early-career scientists with doctoral degrees or equivalent research experience are encouraged to apply. Successful applicants typically demonstrate strong research proposals with clear objectives, feasible methodologies, and potential for significant impact. Proposals that emphasize sustainability, innovation, and community relevance are especially competitive. How to Apply Interested candidates can begin the application process via the official grant website. Applicants are advised to review all eligibility criteria and submission requirements carefully. Preparing a compelling proposal that clearly articulates the research problem, approach, expected outcomes, and broader significance will enhance the chances of success. The Jennifer Ward Oppenheimer Research Grant offers a remarkable opportunity for early-career researchers to secure funding, amplify their work, and contribute to scientific progress in Africa. With its substantial financial support and commitment to impactful research, the JWO Research Grant is a catalyst for innovation and sustainable solutions. Researchers passionate about advancing science for Africa’s future are encouraged to apply before the 1 May 2026 deadline. Deadline : 1 May 2026 APPLY HERE For more information on this grant , click here . For more grant opportunities, click here. title: Globethics Emerging Leaders in Ethical AI Governance Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded): A Global Opportunity to Shape the Future of Responsible AI
summary: The Globethics Emerging Leaders in Ethical AI Governance Fellowship 2026 is a highly competitive, fully funded international programme designed to empower the next generation of leaders in artificial intelligence governance. Organized by Globethics , this fellowship targets early-career professionals who are passionate about ensuring that AI development is ethical, inclusive, and aligned with global human values. As artificial intelligence continues to transform industries and societies, the need for ethical oversight and governance has become more urgent than ever. This fellowship equips participants with the tools, networks, and real-world experience needed to influence policy, innovation, and implementation in AI systems worldwide. About the Fellowship The Globethics fellowship is uniquely structured to bring together professionals from diverse sectors, including academia, government, civil society, and the private sector. It focuses on values-driven AI governance, emphasizing human rights, inclusivity, accountability, and sustainability. Participants will engage in interdisciplinary learning, collaborate with global experts, and contribute to impactful projects that address pressing challenges in AI governance. The programme fosters a global perspective, enabling fellows to understand how ethical AI frameworks can be applied across different cultural and socio-economic contexts. Key Features of the Fellowship One of the standout aspects of this fellowship is its comprehensive approach, combining academic learning, practical application, and international exposure. Phase I: Core Programme Selected fellows will participate in a robust foundational programme that includes: Geneva Residency (6–10 July 2026): Fellows will attend an in-person residency in Geneva, a global hub for diplomacy and international governance. This experience offers direct insight into global policy-making processes. Participation in the AI for Good Global Summit: Fellows will engage with world leaders, innovators, and policymakers shaping the future of AI. Expert Mentorship: Participants will receive guidance from leading experts in AI ethics and governance. Fully Funded Training: Accommodation and programme costs during the residency are covered, removing financial barriers to participation. Project Development: Fellows will conceptualize and develop a research, policy, or innovation project addressing real-world AI governance challenges. Global Networking Opportunities: Participants gain access to an international network of professionals and alumni, with opportunities for collaboration, publication, and visibility. Phase II: Advanced Programme (Competitive Selection) Top-performing fellows from Phase I will advance to a more intensive second phase, which includes: Seed funding to support project implementation Advanced mentorship and technical support Opportunity to present at the Global Ethics Forum (October 2026) Increased exposure to global stakeholders, policymakers, and funders Eligibility Criteria The fellowship is open to early-career professionals aged 24–35 who demonstrate a strong interest or involvement in AI ethics and governance. Eligible applicants may come from: Academia (Master’s, PhD, or postdoctoral researchers) Government and public institutions Private sector (technology, AI, compliance, innovation) Civil society and interfaith organizations Applicants should demonstrate: Active engagement in AI ethics, governance, or responsible innovation Ability to design a research or policy-driven project Interest in intercultural or interfaith dialogue Strong motivation to contribute to ethical AI development Notably, the programme is inclusive and does not require any religious affiliation. Application Process To apply for the fellowship, candidates must submit a single PDF document containing: A CV (maximum of 2 pages) A 500-word statement of interest detailing: Relevant background and experience Motivation for applying Proposed project idea Optional supporting materials such as publications, portfolio, or recommendation letters Applications are submitted online through the official application platform. Important Dates Applications Open: 26 March 2026 Selection Results: By 24 April 2026 Welcome Webinar: June 2026 Geneva Residency: 6–10 July 2026 Project Development Phase: July – September 2026 Why You Should Apply This fellowship goes beyond traditional academic programmes by offering practical, real-world impact opportunities. Participants are not only trained but also empowered to implement solutions that address global AI governance challenges. Key benefits include: Building a strong international network in AI governance Gaining exposure on global platforms Developing impactful projects with real-world applications Enhancing leadership, research, and policy-making skills The opportunity to engage in Geneva further enhances the experience, placing fellows at the center of global governance discussions. About Globethics Globethics is an international non-governmental organization with consultative status at the United Nations Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC). The organization is dedicated to promoting ethical leadership, responsible governance, and sustainable development. Through its initiatives, Globethics plays a critical role in advancing global conversations around ethical AI, human rights, and social justice. Application Deadline: 16 April 2026 APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here For more fellowship opportunities, click here . title: Country Manager Germany (Remote, Munich Preferred) | Lead Impact-Driven Change in Global Education
summary: Are you a strategic leader passionate about transforming lives through education? Street Child Germany e.V. is offering a unique opportunity to step into a high-impact leadership role as Country Manager Germany. This position places you at the forefront of a mission to ensure every child has access to safe and quality education.With operations rooted in Germany and impact spanning vulnerable communities worldwide, this role combines leadership, fundraising, and operational excellence in one dynamic position. About Street Child Germany Street Child is a globally recognized organization dedicated to improving access to education in some of the world’s toughest environments. Its German arm, Street Child Germany e.V., plays a critical role in mobilizing resources and partnerships to support this mission.The German team is small but highly mission-driven, working collaboratively to deliver measurable impact. Role Overview: Country Manager Germany Location: Remote within Germany (Munich area preferred) Start Date: 01 May 2026 Workload: 80–100% As Country Manager, you will lead the organization’s strategy, fundraising, operations, and growth in Germany. Reporting directly to the Board, you will collaborate with international partners to scale impact and drive sustainable funding. Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership Growth Represent and position the organization as a trusted non-profit in Germany Drive visibility, partnerships, and growth opportunities Collaborate with the Board on strategy, governance, and risk Fundraising Partnerships Develop and execute Germany’s fundraising strategy Lead grant applications and donor reporting Build relationships with foundations, corporates, and philanthropists Operations Team Management Oversee finance, HR, administration, and compliance Manage and support the fundraising team Coordinate with the international network Candidate Profile Essential Requirements Must reside in Germany (travel to Munich required if remote) Strong alignment with the organization’s mission Native-level German and strong English proficiency Proven leadership and stakeholder management skills Ability to work independently in a small team environment Highly Desirable Experience in German fundraising (foundations, corporates, philanthropists) Knowledge of institutional donors (BMZ, EU, Auswärtiges Amt) Familiarity with German non-profit law and governance What You’ll Gain A meaningful leadership role in a fast-growing global organization Opportunity to shape the future of Street Child Germany Access to an experienced board and international network Flexible working structure with a Munich-based office Diversity Safeguarding Street Child Germany is committed to diversity and inclusion. Applications are welcome from all qualified individuals regardless of background. All offers are subject to reference checks, criminal record checks, and safeguarding compliance. Application Process Submit your CV and a short motivation letter (in English) via the official application link. Deadline: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis (apply as early as possible). title: BIGS-DR Scholarships 2026 at ZEF, University of Bonn: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Global South Researchers in Sustainable Development
summary: The Center for Development Research (ZEF) at the University of Bonn is inviting applications for the prestigious BIGS-DR Scholarships 2026. This fully funded doctoral opportunity is designed for talented young researchers from the Global South who are passionate about sustainable development and interdisciplinary research. Through its structured doctoral program, the Bonn International Graduate School for Development Research (BIGS-DR), ZEF provides an exceptional academic environment for scholars aiming to address some of the world’s most pressing global challenges. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the BIGS-DR Scholarships is August 31, 2026 . Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their research proposals and supporting documents well in advance to ensure a competitive application. About the Programme The BIGS-DR doctoral programme is an interdisciplinary PhD track that combines academic excellence with practical relevance. It supports students throughout their doctoral journey, from initial coursework to final dissertation. Participants benefit from: Structured doctoral training modules Interdisciplinary research approaches Soft skills and career development training Close supervision by international experts ZEF hosts approximately 50 senior researchers from diverse disciplines, providing a rich intellectual environment where doctoral candidates can thrive and develop innovative research solutions. Research Focus Areas Applicants are expected to submit a creative and impactful research proposal aligned with sustainable development themes. Relevant topics include: Sustainable food systems Agriculture and rural development Nutrition and public health Climate change and environmental sustainability Biodiversity conservation Migration and development Governance and institutional frameworks Conflict and development dynamics The programme strongly encourages interdisciplinary approaches that connect multiple fields to address complex global challenges. Funding and Scholarships The BIGS-DR programme offers EPOS scholarships , funded by the DAAD (Deutscher Akademischer Austauschdienst). These scholarships typically cover: Monthly stipend Travel costs Health insurance Academic and research support The funding aims to enable scholars to focus fully on their research while gaining international academic exposure. Academic Environment and Benefits ZEF is recognized as a leading global institute for development research. As part of the programme, doctoral candidates will: Join a diverse, international cohort of researchers Engage in collaborative and interdisciplinary learning Access world-class academic supervision Participate in workshops, seminars, and global research networks This environment fosters both academic excellence and professional growth, preparing scholars for careers in academia, international organizations, policy-making, and development practice. Why Choose BIGS-DR at ZEF The BIGS-DR programme stands out for its strong integration of research, policy, and practice. It equips scholars with the tools needed to generate impactful knowledge and contribute to sustainable development solutions worldwide. By studying at one of Germany’s top universities and engaging with a global network of researchers, participants gain both academic credibility and practical expertise. title: Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme 2026: Paid Summer Sustainability Opportunity for Undergraduate Students
summary: The Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme (FLP) 2026 is now open for applications, offering a unique paid summer opportunity for current and recent undergraduate students passionate about climate and sustainability. This prestigious programme provides hands-on experience through real-world climate projects, professional development training, and networking opportunities with experts across academia, policy, and industry. Application Deadline and Programme Dates Applications must be submitted by Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 11:59 PM (UK time) . The programme will run from: Start Date: Monday, July 13, 2026 End Date: Friday, September 11, 2026 Participants are entitled to 5 days of annual leave during this period. Programme Structure and Work Experience Participants, known as Project Assistants, will be assigned to specific climate and sustainability projects hosted by Cambridge Zero and its partners. Projects span a wide range of areas, including: Climate policy and adaptation Sustainability research Climate education Innovation and mapping initiatives Environmental health and future housing Project Assistants will: Work collaboratively in professional environments Engage with researchers, policymakers, and stakeholders Contribute to meaningful outputs such as policy briefings, research reports, and communication materials For example, in the Climate Adaptation Policy project, participants will: Develop a policy briefing based on national climate reports Conduct research and stakeholder consultations Potentially contribute to government evidence submissions Professional Development and Career Growth Beyond project work, the Future Leaders Programme includes a structured development component designed to support participants’ long-term careers. Participants will: Explore diverse career pathways in climate and sustainability Reflect on personal strengths and professional goals Receive coaching and mentorship from experts Build a strong peer network within their cohort This combination of practical experience and personal development ensures participants gain valuable skills applicable to future academic pursuits and job opportunities. Key Benefits of the Programme Paid summer work experience in climate and sustainability Opportunity to work on real-world, impactful projects Direct engagement with professionals and experts Structured career development and coaching Access to a network of like-minded peers and alumni Many past participants have highlighted the programme’s role in shaping their careers, helping them build both technical expertise and professional confidence. Salary, Location, and Working Conditions Salary: £13.45 per hour (aligned with the UK real Living Wage) Working Hours: 37 hours per week Location: In-person in Cambridge, United Kingdom Flexibility: Some flexible working arrangements may be considered Participants must be able to work in Cambridge for the duration of the programme. Eligibility Criteria The programme is open to: Current undergraduate students Recent graduates Applicants should demonstrate: Interest in climate and sustainability Strong motivation to learn and contribute Ability to work collaboratively in a professional setting Why You Should Apply The Cambridge Zero Future Leaders Programme stands out as a comprehensive early-career opportunity in the climate field. It not only provides paid work experience but also equips participants with the knowledge, skills, and networks needed to succeed in sustainability careers. By combining hands-on project work with structured professional development, the programme prepares the next generation of climate leaders to tackle global environmental challenges effectively. title: GAIN Mentor Program 2026/2027: Volunteer to Guide African Students into Graduate School (Global Opportunity)
summary: The Graduate Application International Network (GAIN) Mentor Program 2026/2027 is a unique global volunteer opportunity designed for experienced academics and professionals who are passionate about increasing African representation in graduate education. This impactful mentorship initiative connects mentors with aspiring African students, guiding them through the complex graduate application process in fields such as economics, public policy, political science, and public health. By becoming a GAIN mentor, you will play a direct role in shaping the academic and professional futures of talented African students while contributing to a broader mission of equity and inclusion in global academia. About the GAIN Program The GAIN program is a structured, multi-phase initiative that supports African students throughout their graduate application journey. It combines educational webinars, personalized mentorship, and financial support to reduce barriers to higher education. The program is divided into three main components: Webinar Program Mentoring Program Alumni Support Network Each component is designed to progressively equip participants with the knowledge, resources, and confidence needed to successfully apply to graduate schools worldwide. The Role of a GAIN Mentor GAIN mentors are at the heart of the program. They provide personalized guidance and support to mentees navigating the graduate application process. Core Responsibilities Mentors typically: Guide one mentee through the entire application cycle Conduct regular virtual meetings (usually bi-weekly) Provide feedback on application materials such as CVs and statements of purpose Offer advice on selecting universities and programs Support preparation for standardized tests such as GRE and TOEFL Share personal experiences and insights from their academic journeys In addition to one-on-one mentoring, some mentors may choose to: Support group sessions or workshops Offer expertise in specific areas such as scholarship applications or essay editing Participate in collaborative mentor networks or peer-support groups Time Commitment and Duration Mentors are expected to commit a moderate but consistent amount of time throughout the program. Key Details Commitment: Approximately 4–8 hours per month Duration: May/June 2026 – April 2027 Format: Fully virtual engagement This flexible structure allows mentors to balance their professional responsibilities while making a meaningful impact. Who Typically Becomes a GAIN Mentor GAIN mentors are usually individuals who have successfully navigated graduate education themselves and are willing to support others on the same path. Typical Mentor Profiles PhD students Academic researchers University professors Policy professionals and advisors Most mentors have firsthand experience with graduate applications and understand the challenges involved. Eligibility Requirements for Mentors To register as a GAIN mentor, applicants must meet the following criteria: Required Criteria Ability to commit 4–8 hours per month throughout the mentoring cycle Experience completing a graduate application process in: Economics Public policy Political science Public health Or related disciplines Strong interest in increasing African representation in academia Willingness to guide and support mentees consistently Preferred Criteria Previous mentoring experience Strong communication and interpersonal skills Commitment to diversity, inclusion, and academic equity Overview of the GAIN Program Structure Understanding the full GAIN program helps mentors better support their mentees. 1. Webinar Program (January – June) This foundational phase provides participants with essential knowledge about the graduate application process. Topics covered include: Scholarships and funding opportunities GRE, TOEFL, and standardized test strategies Writing academic CVs and statements of purpose Securing letters of recommendation Choosing universities and career paths Balancing academic pursuits with personal responsibilities Webinars are held every two weeks and feature leading African scholars and experts. 2. Mentoring Program This is the core phase where mentors actively engage with mentees. Key features include: One-on-one mentor-mentee matching based on: Field of study Professional background Country of origin Bi-weekly meetings and continuous support Guidance on application preparation and submission The mentoring phase begins with a GRE summer bootcamp, providing participants with access to preparation materials and structured support. 3. Financial Support for Mentees GAIN provides financial assistance to selected participants to reduce barriers to application. Support includes: Application fee coverage Standardized test fees (GRE, TOEFL) Score submission costs High-performing mentees may qualify for Mentoring+, which offers: Increased financial support Enhanced mentorship engagement 4. GAIN Alumni Network After successful admission into graduate programs, mentees transition into the GAIN Alumni Network. Benefits include: Monthly check-in sessions Continued mentorship and guidance Targeted webinars and academic support Community building and networking opportunities This ensures long-term success beyond admission. Why You Should Become a GAIN Mentor Becoming a GAIN mentor is not only an opportunity to give back but also a chance to grow professionally and personally. Key Benefits Contribute to increasing African representation in global academia Support talented students in achieving their academic goals Build meaningful professional relationships Gain mentorship and leadership experience Join a global network of academics and professionals Application Process To become a GAIN mentor: Complete the Online Mentor Registration Form Provide details about your academic and professional background Indicate your availability and areas of expertise Application Deadline The deadline to register as a GAIN Mentor for the 2026/2027 cycle is: April 30, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early to secure placement in the program. Click HERE to learn more about the GAIN Mentor Program 2026/2027. Discover more global Mentorship and African opportunities on OFY. title: Researcher/Writer – Climate Adaptation & Resilience : Global Remote Opportunity with the Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB)
summary: The Institute for Human Rights and Business is currently seeking 1–2 experienced Researchers/Writers specializing in climate adaptation and resilience to lead a new phase of its flagship JUST Stories project. This is a unique global opportunity for professionals who combine rigorous research expertise with exceptional storytelling skills and a deep understanding of climate justice. This role sits at the intersection of climate action, human rights, and global development, offering the chance to contribute to a transformative initiative aimed at reshaping how climate adaptation is understood, financed, and implemented worldwide. Application Deadline and Timeline Applications must be submitted by Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 11:59 PM (UK time) . Shortlisted candidates will be invited to: First-round interviews: April 23–24, 2026 Second-round interviews: Scheduled the following week The expected start date is April or May 2026 , with the contract running through September 2027 . About the JUST Stories Project The JUST Stories initiative, launched in 2024, is a global effort to identify and document real-world examples of effective, people-centered climate action. While the first phase focused on decarbonization, this second phase shifts attention toward climate adaptation and resilience. The project is grounded in a critical insight: climate solutions fail when they exclude the communities most affected. By contrast, successful adaptation emerges from inclusive, locally grounded, and co-designed approaches. Over the next 18 months, the project will: Identify and document community-led adaptation solutions worldwide Highlight best practices and enabling conditions Translate these stories into actionable insights for policymakers, investors, and practitioners These outputs are not merely narratives but serve as evidence-based tools to inform decision-making across finance, policy, and corporate sectors. Role Overview The selected Researchers/Writers will play a central role in shaping this next phase by producing immersive, field-based stories that bridge the gap between local realities and global decision-making systems. The role requires the ability to operate across two critical dimensions: Ground-level engagement with communities and frontline actors System-level understanding of finance, governance, and climate policy Key Responsibilities Research and Analysis Conduct in-depth literature reviews on climate adaptation, resilience, and locally-led approaches Identify high-impact case studies across diverse global contexts Develop frameworks to assess effective and equitable adaptation Fieldwork and Primary Research Design and conduct interviews with community leaders, institutions, and workers Undertake international field visits to gather firsthand data and narratives Ensure ethical research practices, including consent and safeguarding Narrative Development Produce high-quality, publication-ready stories integrating human experience and analytical insight Translate complex findings into clear, actionable recommendations Collaborate with communications teams to create multi-format outputs (articles, briefings, audio, visual content) Strategic Contribution Support cross-sector engagement involving policymakers, investors, and civil society Contribute to synthesizing lessons across case studies Participate in global convenings such as Climate Weeks and COP events Required Expertise and Skills Applicants must demonstrate: At least 7 years of experience in research and writing Proven expertise in climate adaptation and resilience Strong background in qualitative research and fieldwork Exceptional storytelling and analytical writing abilities Understanding of global frameworks such as UN climate and human rights standards Experience in working with Indigenous communities, NGOs, or climate finance actors is highly valued. Contract Details and Compensation Type: Consultancy contract under UK law Duration: April/May 2026 – September 2027 Compensation: Approximately £70,000 total budget Structure Options: One full-time lead researcher/writer Two part-time contributors Candidates must: Have the legal right to work in their country Provide their own equipment and internet access Be available for international travel (up to three times per year) Work Environment This is a fully remote role open globally. However, candidates must be located in time zones compatible with Central European Time (CET) or westward to ensure collaboration with teams operating on UK and West Africa time. Why This Opportunity Matters This role represents more than a writing or research position. It is an opportunity to directly influence how climate adaptation is conceptualized and implemented globally. By documenting and amplifying community-led solutions, the selected candidates will help shift dominant narratives away from top-down approaches toward inclusive, effective, and sustainable climate action. Professionals passionate about climate justice, human rights, and impactful storytelling are strongly encouraged to apply. title: NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants 2026: $2.5 Million Funding Opportunity for Nonprofits Supporting Youth, Storytelling, and Community Engagement
summary: The NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants 2026 program is now officially open, offering a significant funding opportunity for nonprofit organizations working to strengthen local communities. With a total of $2.5 million in grants available, this initiative reflects NBCUniversal’s commitment to empowering grassroots organizations and fostering sustainable community development. This annual grant program is designed to support nonprofit organizations operating in select markets where NBC and Telemundo-owned television stations are present. By providing unrestricted funding, NBCUniversal ensures that recipient organizations have the flexibility to allocate resources where they are most needed, enabling them to maximize their impact. Application Timeline and Deadline Applications for the 2026 NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants will be accepted from March 24, 2026, through April 24, 2026 . All applications must be submitted before 7:59:59 PM Eastern Time on April 24, 2026 . Late submissions will not be considered under any circumstances, making it essential for applicants to plan and submit their proposals well in advance of the deadline. Funding Overview NBCUniversal will award grants to eligible nonprofit organizations with annual operating expenses ranging between $100,000 and $1,000,000 . The grants are categorized as general operating funds , meaning recipients are not restricted to specific project expenditures. Instead, funding can support broader organizational needs, including staffing, program expansion, infrastructure, and operational sustainability. Key Focus Areas The NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants program prioritizes organizations that are actively working within three major thematic areas: 1. Youth Education and Empowerment This category supports programs that equip young people with essential tools for success. Initiatives may include: STEM and STEAM education programs Youth entrepreneurship development In-school and community-based educational support Skill-building and leadership development initiatives Organizations that focus on preparing youth for future academic and professional success are strongly encouraged to apply. 2. Next Generation Storytellers This category is dedicated to nurturing emerging voices and creative talent. Eligible programs include: Media and communications training Arts, journalism, and storytelling initiatives Opportunities in sports and entertainment industries Platforms that amplify youth voices and perspectives This focus area aims to diversify and strengthen the pipeline of talent in storytelling and media industries. 3. Community Engagement Programs under this category aim to strengthen social cohesion and civic participation. Examples include: Volunteerism initiatives Community-building events and programs Projects that encourage local engagement and collaboration Efforts to foster unity and inclusivity within communities Organizations working to create meaningful connections and engagement at the community level will find strong alignment with this category. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants, applicants must: Be a registered nonprofit organization Operate within one of the eligible NBC or Telemundo station markets Have total annual expenses between $100,000 and $1,000,000 Demonstrate measurable impact within their local community Align their work with at least one of the three focus areas Application Process Interested organizations must complete their applications through the official NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants portal. Additionally, applicants are encouraged to register for informational webinars hosted by NBCUniversal to gain insights into the application process, evaluation criteria, and best practices for submission. Applicants should ensure that their proposals clearly articulate their mission, impact, and alignment with NBCUniversal’s priority areas. Strong applications typically highlight measurable outcomes, community relevance, and sustainability. Why This Grant Matters The NBCUniversal Local Impact Grants stand out due to their flexibility and emphasis on local impact. By offering unrestricted funding, the program recognizes the importance of empowering nonprofits to address community needs in dynamic and adaptive ways. Moreover, the focus on youth, storytelling, and community engagement reflects a forward-looking approach to social development—one that values innovation, inclusivity, and grassroots leadership. Organizations seeking to expand their reach, strengthen their operations, or deepen their community impact should strongly consider applying for this opportunity. title: Fully Funded PhD in African Health Economics & Diagnostics at LSE 2026: Complete Guide to the AHEaD Studentship Opportunity
summary: The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE) is offering two highly competitive, fully funded PhD studentships under the Wellcome-funded African Health, Economics and Diagnostics (AHEaD) Project. This prestigious doctoral opportunity is designed for candidates who are passionate about advancing research at the intersection of health systems, economics, and diagnostic technologies, particularly within African contexts. This studentship goes beyond a traditional PhD. It integrates rigorous academic training with real-world policy research, offering candidates the chance to contribute directly to a major international research programme shaping the future of diagnostics and healthcare systems across Africa. About the AHEaD Project The African Health, Economics and Diagnostics (AHEaD) Project is a major research initiative funded by Wellcome, focused on understanding and improving the role of diagnostics in health systems across Africa. Diagnostics, particularly point-of-care tests, are critical in improving early detection, treatment decisions, and overall health outcomes. However, their accessibility, affordability, and effectiveness are influenced by complex economic, political, and systemic factors. The AHEaD project seeks to address these challenges by examining: How diagnostic tools are developed, produced, and distributed The economic and health impacts of diagnostic technologies Policy frameworks that influence diagnostic markets Barriers to access and adoption across different health systems PhD candidates will play a central role in this research, contributing to evidence-based insights that can influence policy, markets, and healthcare delivery systems. Programme Overview Successful applicants will be enrolled in the MPhil/PhD in Health Policy at LSE while working closely with the AHEaD research team. Key Programme Details Institution: London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE) Department: LSE Health Department of Health Policy Programme: MPhil/PhD in Health Policy Start Date: September 2026 Duration: 4 years (full-time) Funding: Fully funded (home and overseas tuition covered) Deadline: April 27, 2026 (5:00 PM) This dual structure ensures that candidates receive both world-class academic training and hands-on research experience. Research Scope and Thematic Areas The AHEaD PhD studentships support a wide range of research topics within the economics of diagnostics. Candidates are encouraged to develop innovative, interdisciplinary research proposals aligned with the project’s goals. Key Research Areas Political Economy of Diagnostics Manufacturing systems and supply chains Market dynamics and governance structures Institutional influences on diagnostic availability Health and Economic Impact Cost-effectiveness of diagnostic technologies Macroeconomic benefits of local production Impact on healthcare delivery and outcomes Health Technology Assessment (HTA) Evaluation of diagnostic tools Reimbursement and pricing models Policy implications for adoption and scale-up Financing and Regulatory Frameworks Investment strategies in diagnostics Regulatory policies and incentives Sustainable market development Access and Uptake of Diagnostics Affordability challenges Behavioral and systemic barriers Data integration and digital health systems While the research has a strong African focus, applicants are encouraged to incorporate comparative or global perspectives where relevant. Structure of the PhD Programme The four-year doctoral programme is carefully structured to balance coursework, independent research, and active participation in the AHEaD project. Year 1: Foundation and Research Development Completion of required coursework in health policy and economics Development and refinement of PhD research topic Early engagement with AHEaD research activities Years 2–4: Advanced Research and Fieldwork Full-time doctoral research Fieldwork and primary data collection (including international travel) Collaboration with global research partners Contribution to AHEaD project outputs Ongoing Academic Engagement Throughout the programme, candidates will: Meet regularly with supervisors and project managers Participate in seminars, workshops, and academic discussions Contribute to collaborative research initiatives Engage in interdisciplinary learning and networking Funding and Financial Support One of the most attractive aspects of this opportunity is its comprehensive financial package. What the Funding Covers Full tuition fees for both home and international students Research Assistant role with additional income (up to approximately 20 hours per week) Funding for travel related to fieldwork and data collection Support for attending conferences and engaging with global research networks This ensures that candidates can focus fully on their research without financial constraints. Roles and Responsibilities of PhD Candidates PhD students under the AHEaD programme will take on multiple roles as researchers, collaborators, and contributors to a broader research ecosystem. Key Responsibilities Conduct independent doctoral research aligned with AHEaD objectives Support literature reviews and evidence synthesis Participate in data collection and empirical analysis Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams Contribute to policy-relevant research outputs Candidates are expected to demonstrate initiative, intellectual curiosity, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. Eligibility Criteria and Person Specification The programme seeks highly motivated candidates with strong academic backgrounds and a clear interest in health economics and policy. Essential Requirements Master’s degree in economics, political economy, or a related field Strong analytical and quantitative skills Experience with economic or policy-relevant datasets Demonstrated interest in diagnostics, health systems, or public policy Excellent academic writing and communication skills Ability to work independently and within teams Additional Requirements Willingness to reside in the UK for the duration of the programme Availability for international travel (1–2 months at a time) Strong problem-solving and research capabilities Desirable Qualifications Experience working in African contexts (e.g., Senegal, Ethiopia) French language proficiency Background in econometrics, HTA, or applied economics Knowledge of diagnostic, pharmaceutical, or healthcare markets Entry Requirements Applicants must meet LSE’s PhD entry requirements, including: A Master’s degree with a minimum average equivalent to 65% A dissertation mark of at least 65% Importantly, prior expertise in diagnostics is not required, making this opportunity accessible to candidates from diverse academic and professional backgrounds. Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide To apply for the AHEaD PhD studentship, candidates must complete a Formal Application to LSE. Steps to Apply Apply to the MPhil/PhD in Health Policy at LSE Clearly indicate “Economics of Diagnostics: AHEaD” in your application Submit a research proposal (maximum 1,000 words) Writing a Strong Research Proposal The research proposal is a critical component of the application and should demonstrate: Identification of a clear and relevant research gap Justification of the topic’s importance and timeliness Identification of key stakeholders Clear methodology and data sources Potential policy or system-level impact Applicants should aim for conceptual clarity, methodological rigor, and alignment with micro, meso, or macro-level analysis. Application Deadline The final deadline to submit your application is: April 27, 2026 (5:00 PM) Late submissions are not accepted, and early preparation is strongly advised. Why This Opportunity Stands Out This PhD studentship is not just an academic programme—it is a career-defining opportunity. Key Advantages Study at one of the world’s leading institutions Work on high-impact, policy-relevant research Gain international exposure and collaboration opportunities Build expertise in a rapidly growing field Contribute to improving healthcare systems across Africa Contact Information For informal enquiries, applicants can contact: Lesong Conteh – l.conteh@lse.ac.uk Click HERE to learn more about the PhD Studentships in African Health, Economics and Diagnostics at The London School of Economics and Political Science. Discover more global PhD Scholarship and Research opportunities on OFY . The post Fully Funded PhD in African Health Economics Diagnostics at LSE 2026: Complete Guide to the AHEaD Studentship Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Regional Focal Point (APAC) at SDSN Youth 2026: Fully Remote Global Leadership Opportunity in Sustainable Development
summary: The SDSN Youth Regional Focal Point (RFP) for Asia-Pacific (APAC) is a highly impactful global leadership opportunity designed for passionate young leaders committed to advancing sustainable development. Hosted under the umbrella of the UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN), this role places you at the heart of international collaboration, policy engagement, and youth-driven innovation aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and the Paris Climate Agreement. This fully remote position offers an unparalleled platform to work with global stakeholders, coordinate youth-led initiatives, and contribute meaningfully to solving some of the world’s most pressing environmental, social, and economic challenges. About SDSN: A Global Network for Sustainable Solutions Established in 2012 under the auspices of the United Nations Secretary-General, SDSN mobilizes scientific and technological expertise worldwide to promote practical solutions for sustainable development. The organization plays a pivotal role in: Advancing the implementation of the SDGs Supporting climate action under the Paris Agreement Promoting integrated approaches to global challenges Facilitating knowledge exchange across countries and sectors About SDSN Youth: Empowering the Next Generation of Changemakers Launched in 2015, SDSN Youth is a flagship initiative focused on empowering young people globally to lead sustainable development efforts. Its core mission revolves around: Building youth capacity through education and training Promoting awareness of sustainable development issues Supporting youth-led innovation and advocacy Encouraging collaboration across global networks Through its extensive network of youth-led organizations, SDSN Youth has become a powerful force in mobilizing young leaders worldwide. Understanding the Youth Networks Program The Youth Networks Program is a cornerstone of SDSN Youth’s global strategy. It connects young leaders through national and regional networks, enabling them to: Develop skills and knowledge related to sustainability Collaborate with governments, NGOs, and private sector stakeholders Launch and scale impactful sustainable development initiatives Each network is coordinated locally by National Coordinators (NCs) and regionally by Regional Focal Points (RFPs), ensuring alignment with global sustainability goals. Role Overview: Regional Focal Point (APAC) As a Regional Focal Point, you will serve as the key liaison between SDSN Youth’s global leadership and regional youth networks across the Asia-Pacific region. This role requires strong leadership, communication, and coordination skills to support and guide National Coordinators effectively. Key Responsibilities Leadership and Coordination Act as the primary point of contact between SDSN Youth and regional networks Support National Coordinators in project management and strategic planning Foster collaboration and knowledge-sharing across countries Monitoring and Reporting Track and evaluate activities within regional networks Ensure alignment with SDGs and SDSN Youth objectives Report regional progress to global leadership teams Communication and Engagement Organize monthly calls with National Coordinators Participate in global and regional meetings Maintain consistent communication across time zones Strategic Development Identify growth opportunities within the region Support recruitment of new National Coordinators Promote SDSN Youth initiatives to relevant stakeholders Eligibility Criteria: Who Can Apply? This opportunity is specifically designed for young leaders who meet the following criteria: Age Requirement Applicants must be between 18 and 30 years old Geographic Eligibility Must be based in the Asia-Pacific (APAC) region Must remain in the region for at least 12 months after application Educational and Professional Background Experience in youth leadership, student organizations, or volunteer work Background in project coordination, event management, or stakeholder engagement is highly desirable Skills and Competencies Excellent command of the English language (written and spoken) Strong organizational and communication skills Familiarity with tools like Google Workspace Ability to work independently in a remote, multicultural environment Additional Advantages Prior involvement with SDSN or SDSN Youth networks Connections with youth organizations or sustainability initiatives Commitment and Working Conditions Duration Initial term: 12 months Includes a 3-month trial period إمكانية التمديد based on performance Time Commitment Minimum of 10 hours per week Work Setup Fully remote (global virtual environment) Requires flexibility to work across multiple time zones Compensation This is a voluntary (unpaid) position Benefits: Why You Should Apply Despite being a voluntary role, the SDSN Youth Regional Focal Point position offers substantial professional and personal development opportunities: Global Exposure Represent SDSN Youth at international events Engage with global stakeholders across sectors Skill Development Gain hands-on experience in leadership, coordination, and strategy Access training programs and capacity-building resources Career Advancement Build a strong personal brand in sustainability Enhance your resume with a globally recognized organization Networking Opportunities Collaborate with professionals from governments, NGOs, and the private sector Join a vibrant global community of changemakers Ideal Candidates: Who Should Consider This Role? This opportunity is best suited for: Young professionals passionate about sustainable development and climate action Emerging leaders seeking international exposure and impact Individuals interested in policy, advocacy, and global collaboration Students or graduates looking to build a career in development, sustainability, or international relations Application Process Candidates are encouraged to apply through official platforms such as: Indeed Resume submission LinkedIn application portal Given the competitive nature of this role and the lack of a fixed deadline, early application is highly recommended. Apply here Deadline Not explicitly specified (applications expected to be reviewed on a rolling basis; early application strongly recommended). The SDSN Youth Regional Focal Point (APAC) role is more than just a volunteer position—it is a gateway to global leadership in sustainable development. If you are driven by purpose, eager to lead, and committed to shaping a better future, this opportunity offers the perfect platform to make a meaningful impact. Take the next step in your leadership journey and become part of a global movement working toward the 2030 Agenda.
